worksheets.title: Quick check: rights vs wants
worksheets.instruction: A legal right is protected by law; a want is something you would like but are not entitled to. Sort each item.

items.text: A fair trial if you are accused of a crime

1.instruction: A right is something the law protects for everyone. A want is something you would like. Tap right or want for each item.

6.instruction: Classify each item as a legal right (guaranteed by law) or a personal want (a preference, not a legal entitlement).

worksheets.submitMessage: Good. Rights protect your freedom, safety and dignity — wants are choices, not legal guarantees.

worksheets.title: Quick check: discrimination
worksheets.instruction: Direct discrimination treats someone worse because of who they are. Indirect discrimination is a neutral rule that unfairly disadvantages a group. Sort each example.

items.text: A shop refuses to hire someone because of their race
A rule that all staff must work Saturdays, excluding some religious groups
A club bans women from becoming members
A 'no headwear' policy that disadvantages some faiths

1.instruction: Direct means someone is treated worse on purpose because of who they are. Indirect means a rule that quietly disadvantages a group. Tap one for each.

6.instruction: Decide whether each example is direct discrimination (explicit unfair treatment) or indirect discrimination (a neutral rule with an unfair effect).

worksheets.submitMessage: Well done. The Equality Act 2010 protects people from both direct and indirect discrimination.

worksheets.title: Quick check: rights and responsibilities
worksheets.instruction: Some things the law gives you (rights); others are things you are expected to do (responsibilities). Sort each one.

items.text: Sharing your opinions through free speech
Paying taxes to fund public services
Serving on a jury when asked

1.instruction: A right is something the law gives you. A responsibility is something you are expected to do. Tap one for each line.

6.instruction: Classify each item as a right you hold or a responsibility you are expected to meet as a citizen.

worksheets.submitMessage: Great thinking. Rights and responsibilities work together to keep society fair.
